lineage: Life and Works of Rufinus with Jerome's Apology Against Rufinus.
Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.

A criticism on Rufinus' Apology to Anastasius. His excuses for not coming to Rome are absurd. His parents are dead and the journey is easy. No one ever heard before of his being imprisoned or exiled for the faith.

metadata.author: Life and Works of Rufinus with Jerome's Apology Against Rufinus

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: Thus far I have made answer about my crimes, and indeed in defence of my crimes, which my crafty encomiast formerly urged against me, and which his disciples still constantly press.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: I have done so not as well as I ought but as I was able, putting a check upon my complaints, for my object has been not so much to accuse others as to defend myself.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: I will now come to his Apology, by which he strives to justify himself to Anastasius, Bishop of the City of Rome, and, in order to defend himself, constructs a mass of calumnies against me.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: His love for me is like that which a man who has been carried away by the tempest and nearly drowned in deep water feels for the strong swimmer at whose foot he clutches: he is determined that I shall sink or swim with him.
